summary.intent: Fix 5 non-Gregorian calendar fidelity bugs in the DateTimePicker component identified by a Codex pass-3 review, covering era handling, dynamic month ranges, date reprojection, and disabled hidden input.
summary.outcome: All 5 pass-3 findings were implemented and tested: era-aware date assembly, calendar-aware month range clamping, date reprojection in maybe_publish/apply_value, and disabled hidden input propagation. 136 tests pass, all 10 CI gates green, changes committed and pushed.

repo: DateTimePicker uses helpers: current_era_for_assembly, max_months_in_year, days_in_month, CalendarDate::era, and default_era for non-Gregorian calendar support
refresh_day_range was renamed to refresh_date_ranges to also handle calendar-aware month range updates
The spec at spec/components/date-time/date-time-picker.md must be kept in sync with implementation changes to assemble_date and helper functions
Codex review is run in multiple passes (pass-1, pass-2, pass-3) with threads that need to be replied to and resolved after each fix batch

code.finding: assemble_date now takes era parameter via current_era helper; project_date helper added near clamp_datetime for reprojection in maybe_publish and apply_value
hidden_input_attrs must propagate ctx.disabled to the hidden input element for form submission correctness
Tests added for: Hebrew 5784 month max=13, Japanese era preservation, date reprojection, and disabled hidden input — total test count reached 136
Spec updated to document era parameter in assemble_date and added notes about dynamic ranges and reprojection in the helpers section

workflow: Codex multi-pass review workflow: poll for verdict, fetch threads, implement fixes, add tests, sync spec, run full CI gate, commit/push, reply/resolve threads, re-trigger Codex
xci-fast is the fast CI gate command with 10 sub-gates; build must pass before running tests
TodoWrite is used to track each Codex finding as a task item throughout implementation

friction: Multiple sequential Read calls were needed to locate the exact line ranges for assemble_date, maybe_publish, apply_value, and hidden_input_attrs before editing — the file is large enough that targeted reads were required
refresh_day_range had multiple call sites (rebuild_date_segments, init) that needed manual renaming after the method was extended and renamed
open_items: Codex pass-4 may surface additional findings after the pass-3 fixes are reviewed — re-trigger was queued at end of session
Era handling and month-range clamping were added reactively; a proactive audit of other calendar-aware paths in the codebase may still be warranted
